The system is a transactional backend.
The system processes ATM cash withdrawal requests.
The system processes withdrawal requests securely.
The system has a minimal administrative console.
The system enforces balance rules.
The system enforces limit rules.

The system declines withdrawals exceeding the account balance.

The system rolls back all steps completely if any step fails.

The system records every transaction state change with a timestamp.
The ATM Withdrawal Safety Backend is a transactional processing system that handles cash requests from automated teller machines while ensuring security and data integrity.
This backend enforces balance and daily withdrawal limits, rolls back any failed transaction completely, and flags suspicious activity for review.
Users must present a valid card and correct PIN to initiate a withdrawal; after three wrong attempts their account becomes locked.
When a withdrawal request succeeds, the account balance is decremented and cash is dispensed; if any part fails, the entire transaction is undone preserving the original balance.
A secure settlement engine for cash disbursements that validates credentials, enforces spending caps, and audits anomalous patterns.
